resources.string: A sequence assigns each natural number n a term a‚Çô; it‚Äôs a function on ‚Ñï. Terms: a‚ÇÅ, a‚ÇÇ, a‚ÇÉ‚Ä¶

Finite ‚Üí first n terms; Infinite ‚Üí all n. Defined by formula a‚Çô=f(n) or recurrence rule (e.g. Fibonacci: a‚ÇÅ=1,a‚ÇÇ=1,a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÇ=a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ+a‚Çô). Increasing: a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ>a‚Çô; Decreasing: a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ<a‚Çô; Constant ‚Üí equal terms.
Arithmetic progression (A.P.): each term = previous term + constant d.
Common difference d=a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ‚àía‚Çô. Property: a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ=(a‚Çô+a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÇ)/2. nth term: a‚Çô=a‚ÇÅ+(n‚àí1)d. Sum: S‚Çô=(a‚ÇÅ+a‚Çô)n/2.
Geometric progression (G.P.): each term = previous term √ó constant q.
Common ratio q=a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ/a‚Çô. Property: a‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ=‚àö(a‚Çôa‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÇ). nth term: a‚Çô=a‚ÇÅq‚Åø‚Åª¬π. Sum: S‚Çô=a‚ÇÅ(q‚Åø‚àí1)/(q‚àí1), q‚âÝ1.
Problems on Progressions: apply arithmetic and geometric formulas to solve number problems.

Dual-nature: only equal terms (q=1). Insert arithmetic means: e.g., 3 through 19 ‚Üí d=(19‚àí3)/4=4 ‚Üí 3,7,11,15,19. Natural sums: e.g., 2-digit numbers sum=4905. Insert geometric means: 1 through 256 ‚Üí q=4 ‚Üí 1,1*4,4*4,16*4,64*4. Sequence change: G.P. may become A.P. if term adjusted.
Taking Logarithms and Antilogarithms: simplify algebraic expressions using log laws.
log‚Çê(ab)=log‚Çêa+log‚Çêb. log‚Çê(a/b)=log‚Çêa‚àílog‚Çêb. log‚Çê(x·µè)=k¬∑log‚Çêx. Based on a^(log‚Çêb)=b identity.
